conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Jonathan is building SlipIQ, an AI-powered sports prop analytics app with an accompanying Discord community. The project has a fully designed backend architecture and is actively in development, with several core files already built and a phased roadmap in place.

**Personal context**

Jonathan follows professional sports closely, with active interest in MLB, NBA, and NFL. He engages with sports data analytically — tracking standings, lineups, player performance, and betting lines — in ways that directly inform SlipIQ's development.

**Top of mind**

Jonathan is deep in SlipIQ development, with the MLB pitcher strikeout model (Phase 1) as the immediate priority given summer MLB volume. Recent conversations also show active interest in live sports data — MLB and NBA specifically — likely in the context of testing or informing SlipIQ's data pipelines and analytics outputs.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

**SlipIQ architecture and build progress:** Jonathan has designed SlipIQ's full system across multiple sessions. Files already built include `slipiq_engine.py` (projection model), `slipiq_data.py` (BallDontLie + Odds API integration), and `slipiq_writer.py` (Claude API write-up generator). Files still to build include `slipiq_confidence_agent.py` (agentic confidence scoring), `slipiq_lines.py` (hit rates + alt lines), `slipiq_curate.py` (morning curation), `slipiq_slip_review.py` (6-step checklist), `slipiq_discord.py` (Discord formatter), `slipiq_mlb_data.py` (Baseball Savant + FanGraphs), `slipiq_pitcher_model.py` (strikeout distribution), and `slipiq_sharp_review_agent.py`.

**Key product decisions finalized:** The post-mortem feature is branded "The Sharp Review" on Discord and "Game Debrief" on the operator dashboard. Confidence scoring is agentic AI and displays as a percentage to users. Hit rate and confidence % are both visible on pick cards; EV score and raw confidence inputs are visible to Jonathan only. The grade label is derived from a combination of hit rate and confidence.

**Discord server structure:** Free tier includes `#daily-best-pick`, `#results-public`, and `#general-chat`. VIP tier covers sport-specific prop channels (`#mlb-pitcher-props`, `#nba-props`, `#nfl-props`), plus `#sgp-analysis`, `#slip-builder`, `#pre-game-alerts`, `#live-tracker`, `#results-vip`, and `#bankroll-management`. Analysis channels include `#the-sharp-review`, `#model-updates`, and `#weekly-recap`. Community channels include `#wins-board`, `#discussion`, and `#feedback`.

**Build phasing:** Phase 1 — MLB pitcher strikeout model (primary track record engine, active all summer). NBA skeleton parked until October; NFL skeleton parked until September. Phase 2 — MLB hitter props. Phase 3 — SGP Correlation Agent + NFL live. Phase 4 — SGP pricing model + arb engine.

**18 agentic agents designed:** Confidence, Pre-Game Narrative, Slip Construction, Line Shopping, Bankroll Management, CLV Tracking, Discord Content, User Behavior, Injury Impact, Backtesting/Sharp Review, Morning Orchestrator, Slate Triage, Research Compression, Devil's Advocate, Consensus Cross-Check, Post-Game (Sharp Review), SGP Correlation, and Tilt & Wellness.

**Sports data queries (early May 2026):** Jonathan pulled live MLB and NBA data — including Nationals vs. Marlins matchup analysis and 76ers playoff lineup/availability information — likely in the context of evaluating sports data tool outputs relevant to SlipIQ's data layer. Claude used a `fetch_sports_data` tool with parameters for league, team, data type, and game ID; a two-step approach (scores → game ID → game stats) was most effective for granular player data. Head-to-head historical MLB data was not reliably surfaced and may require a dedicated query type or separate source.
